channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Shuhei Yoshida Explains Why Steam Machine is ‘Hard To Recommend’
item.content:encoded:
The Steam Machine is another Valve console in its growing lineup, complementing the Steam Deck. This one is more focused on being a state-of-the-art home console system, and the legendary Shuhei Yoshida has offered his thoughts after spending time with the device.

In a landscape of rising console prices and worrying industry trends—such as Sony stopping disc production for new PlayStation Games in 2028—the Steam Machine is looking to throw its name into the hat.

Shuhei Yoshida, one of the industry’s most respected figures, has offered a mini-review of sorts.

Steam Machine Has Pros and Cons

In a post on X, Yoshida revealed that he had spent a few hours with the Steam Machine and had some good things to say, whilst being objective in some criticism.

On the Nice list, Yoshida said the Steam Machine’s UI is ‘easy to use,’ and ‘being able to boot up by pressing a button on Steam Controller is a killer feature.’

Some of the other positives seem less important, such as customizable face plates and boot-up videos, but being able to play Steam games properly on a big TV is a plus.

  • More Insider Gaming: Star Wars Eclipse Development Is on Track, Says Quantic Dream

However, some of the negatives quickly fight back. Yoshida explained that ‘3D performance is just…meh,’ that ‘The system recommends to default to 1080p – am I going back to PS4 days?’ and ‘Some games take a looooooong time to boot.’

But his final verdict was quite interesting:

‘The price was very unfriendly. Hard to recommend to people unless for research.’

There are rumors that the PS6 might cost around a grand, but we know for a fact that the Steam Machine will cost more than $1,000.

It’s also interesting that Valve has seemingly walked back on Steam Machine 4K60 claims.

It’s a high price tag, for sure. Check out the Steam Machine announcement video below to see what you make of it.

Is the price point going to be a huge stumbling block for the Steam Machine? Let us know via the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming content, read about Onimusha: Way of the Sword launching earlier than planned, and be sure to sign up for our free Weekly Newsletter.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: Activision: Call of Duty League’s Major 4 Broke Two Massive Records
item.content:encoded:
The Call of Duty League’s Major 4 tournament has wrapped, and OpTic Texas walked away with the gorgeous trophy after defeating LA Thieves in a close grand final. It was a monumental event that saw more people attend the arena than ever before, setting a new record for a Call of Duty LAN event, and it set the bar for whatever comes next.

Talking to Activision’s Call of Duty League representatives about the event, I learned that both the number of people in the arena and the number of viewers from around the world topped anything that has ever come before, reflecting the power of an EU-based COD event.

We Need More European Call of Duty Events

The Call of Duty League Major that landed in Paris this past weekend was an incredibly successful event. The crowd was on fire, the competitors were revelling in the atmosphere, and the gameplay was unreal. Every series was a banger, and we saw some major updates take place on that stage in Paris.

Thanks to the Paris Gentle Mates and the organization’s following, this was, by footfall, the biggest Call of Duty event in history, and it’s an indication of the love Europe has for the COD esports scene.

In a statement, the CDL’s representatives explained how two records were broken this past weekend:

Major IV in Paris was a memorable event for the Call of Duty League. More than 40,000 attendees came through the event across the weekend, making it the most attended Call of Duty esports event in history. Thanks to the Paris Gentle Mates, everyone in the arena and viewers around the world were part of an unforgettable experience that also set a new CDL peak viewership record with more than 378,000 concurrent viewers.

The atmosphere throughout the event was electric, and it was incredible to see so many clips shared online of fans celebrating every match and embracing the League with so much energy. We’re excited to carry that momentum into Championship Weekend in Las Vegas as we close out the season and look ahead to what’s next with Modern Warfare 4.

In just a few days, teams will be heading to Las Vegas to compete in the Call of Duty League Championship, the most important event in the annual calendar. Unfortunately, the schedule isn’t the best for European fans, but those with the dedication to tune in will still find time to do so.

  • GIVEAWAY: Win a Championship Gaming Chair with Insider Gaming Esports

Did you enjoy Major 4 in Paris? Let us know what you thought about the event on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the trick that OpTic Texas pulled against LA Thieves, and for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: Today’s Contexto: Hints & Answer For July 2, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Contexto is a word association game that gives you a new word to guess every single day. With every attempt, the AI algorithm determines how close your guess is to the daily answer based on thematic and contextual similarity.

Sometimes, though, no matter how experienced you are, the word you’re looking for can be incredibly vague and hard to deduce. Luckily for you, this guide is full of hints to give you a helping hand, but make sure you give today’s Contexto a go if you haven’t already!

How To Play Contexto

Contexto is incredibly simple to play, but difficult to master. All you have to do is type words into the search bar, and the AI algorithm will tell you how close your word is to the answer.

You’ll know how close your guess is based on the colour of the bar and the number next to it.

  • Red includes guesses with the number 1,501 and above, meaning you’re way off from the secret word.
  • Orange includes guesses between 301 and 1,500, meaning you’re getting closer, but still some ways off.
  • Green includes guesses between 1 and 300, meaning you’re on the right track.

Best Contexto Tips & Tricks

  • Start off using broad terms, then narrow down once you’ve got under the 1,000 mark.
  • Try using Nouns first, and dip into verbs or adjectives once you’ve discovered the context.
  • Experiment with random words at times, especially as different forms of a root word can rank differently, such as work and worker.
  • Guess away, as unlike other word games like Wordle, you aren’t limited to a strict number of attempts.

Best Starting Words For Contexto

Here are some great starting words to get you going with Contexto:

  • Thing
  • Person
  • Place
  • Animal
  • Work
  • Nature
  • Game
  • Concept
  • Idea
  • Food

Contexto Hints For Today, July 2, 2026

Without giving away today’s Contexto answer, here are a few helpful hints to get you thinking in the right direction:

  • Hint 1: Another term for baby
  • Hint 2: Specifically for the first 28 days of its life
  • Hint 3: Newly born

If they don’t help, then here are the nine closest words, going from tenth to second:

  • Breastfeed
  • Neonatal
  • Maternity
  • Mother
  • Child
  • Pregnancy
  • Toddler
  • Baby
  • Infant

Today’s Contexto Answer For July 2, 2026

If you’ve tried everything and still can’t figure out today’s Contexto answer, then you can find it down below:

Answer: Newborn

A Newborn is a baby that was born recently, spanning the first 28 days of life.

When Is The Next Contexto Game?

Contexto is updated daily at 12 AM or Midnight in your respective time zone, meaning the next game will start on July 3.

The Contexto schedule is very consistent, with a new game releasing at the same time every day.

How did you find today’s Contexto? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: When is The DC Update in Fortnite? Leaked Release Date & Content
item.content:encoded:
Fortnite is still in the midst of Chapter 7, Season 3, and while there are still plenty of weeks left to complete your Sprite collection, a shake-up is around the corner with the leaked DC update.

Epic Games has become the undisputed master of collabs, and having continued to benefit from close ties with Disney for the likes of Marvel and Star Wars, it’s time for DC characters to step into the limelight.

For all the details on when the world of DC takes over in Fortnite, Insider Gaming has you covered.

When is The DC Update in Fortnite?

The DC update is set to arrive on July 16, according to prominent leakers, bringing a range of superhero-themed antics to the party.

ShiinaBR cites NotPaloIntel as stating the DC summer update will arrive on July 16, and big reveals could be uncovered starting with next week’s update.

  • Related: All Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 3 Sprites, Effects & Rarities

The return of the likes of Batman, Harley Quinn, and Poison Ivy was even leaked on the Nintendo eShop, and while Epic Games is yet to publicly make an announcement, a full reveal seems imminent.

However, it won’t be a new, DC-themed season, but will instead be an update to the current season, lasting until the end of Chapter 7, Season 3 in August.

What’s Coming in The Fortnite DC Update?

While the full details of the DC update in Fortnite haven’t been revealed by Epic Games, a slew of leaks have indicated what fans can expect to see.

New and returning skins from fan-favorite DC characters are on the agenda, including Batman, Catwoman, Harley Quinn, and Poison Ivy, while there will also be a new Batman Sprite to add to your collection and hunt down the different variants.

  • Related: How to Get a Grim Reaper Sprite in Fortnite Chapter 7, Season 3

The new DC skins are expected to be summer-themed, and there could also be new in-game items added to the loot pool. However, this is not confirmed.

Finally, leaks also show a new Batmobile vehicle will be available alongside a Batman-dog Sidekick, so there’s sset to be plenty to spend your V-Bucks on.

Check out the Olivia Rodrigo Fortnite reveal video below.

Have you purchased the Olivia Rodrigo bundle in Fortnite? Let us know in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For your next read, take a look at how to get a Boss Sprite easily in Fortnite, and don’t forget to subscribe to our newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/fortnite-dc-summer-update/

item.title: When is The Aniimo Closed Beta? Exact Start Time & End Date
item.content:encoded:
It’s been a great year for fans of cozy games, and while there’s still a while to go before a full release, the latest Aniimo closed beta is right around the corner.

Pawprint Studios’ creature-capture title has already garnered the attention of millions of fans worldwide, receiving over 15 million pre-registrations.

For all the details on the Aniimo closed beta, Insider Gaming has you covered.

When Does The Aniimo Closed Beta Start?

The Aniimo Closed Beta begins on Thursday, July 9, but no exact start time has been shared by Pawprint Studio yet. However, more details are imminent.

Sign-ups for the closed beta are available until July 5, and once that date passes, invitations for those selected to participate should start to be distributed.

  • Related: How to Sign Up For The Aniimo Closed Beta

That’s also when players can expect to discover the full details of exactly when the closed beta kicks off, but there may already be a hint of the start time.

Pawprint Studio has confirmed the Aniimo Closed Beta begins on July 9 in the CST time zone, eight hours ahead of UTC, so the doors could open on July 8 for those elsewhere in the world.

When Does The Aniimo Closed Beta End?

No end date has been shared by Pawprint Studio ahead of the start of the closed beta, with precise details expected to follow shortly.

The last closed beta began on January 23, 2026, and ended on February 8, 2026, so a similar duration could take place this time around. However, the end date may not be announced immediately.

The end of the last closed beta was announced on February 6, just two days before the servers closed, so there might not be much warning before the end.

However, all progress is wiped anyway, so you don’t have to worry too much.

Check out the Aniimo closed beta trailer below.

Are you excited for the release of Aniimo? Let us know in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For your next read, check out the free update in Mario Kart World, and don’t forget to sign up for our Insider Gaming Newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: NYT Connections Hints Today (#1117)—Answers for Thursday, July 2
item.content:encoded:
It’s time to add another notch to that NYT Connections streak. In this guide, you’ll find the NYT Connections hints and solutions you need to power through the puzzle and come out the other side without too much fuss. If you’re stuck on today’s NYT Connections challenge, simply refer to the hints below!

Scroll down to get the NYT Connections answers you need!

How to Play NYT Connections

In June 2023, NYT Connections emerged as yet another game in the ever-growing lineup of the New York Times titles. This title soared immediately, and it’s easy to see why. As a free-to-play, accessible New York Times game, NYT Connections is available worldwide and can be played on desktop devices or in a mobile app.

But how do you play NYT Connections?

The NYT team will provide you with a grid of 16 words once a day. The only task left up to you is to sort those words into four groups of four by finding their commonalities. Every NYT Connections puzzle has just one solution, and some words can have double meanings, so it can sometimes be tricky to figure out the answers.

It’s also important to stress that the words are color-coded: yellow, blue, green, and purple. That’s in order of difficulty, from least to most.

What’s critical here is that you get four guesses every day, and if you use those, your NYT Connections answers for today will be out of reach, as it’ll be game over.

Today’s NYT Connections Hints (July 2)

Here are the words for today’s NYT Connections:

  • MOCKINGBIRD
  • FIELD MOUSE
  • TOM-TOM
  • COURT JESTER
  • MIME
  • LOOKING GLASS
  • TRACK RECORD
  • BILLY GOAT
  • DIAMOND RING
  • SPECTACLES
  • DAN DAN NOODLES
  • COPYCAT
  • TALKIE
  • RICH TEXT
  • T-1000
  • WATER CLOSET

And here are the hints I can offer for today’s NYT Connections:

  • Yellow – Things that impersonate other things
  • Green – Older terms for things that we use today
  • Blue – Terms that start with shortened male names
  • Purple – Terms that start with words for sporting venues

Here’s the final warning before we get to the NYT Connections answers for today, July 2!

Are you sure you want to scroll further?

Very well!

NYT Connections Answers Explained for Today, July 2 (#1117)

If you want to jump straight to the answers for today’s NYT Connections puzzle, I’ve got you covered:

  • Yellow – COPYCAT, MIME, MOCKINGBIRD, T-1000
  • Green – LOOKING GLASS, SPECTACLES, TALKIE, WATER CLOSET
  • Blue – BILLY GOAT, DAN DAN NOODLES, RICH TEXT, TOM-TOM
  • Purple – COURT JESTER, DIAMOND RING, FIELD MOUSE, TRACK RECORD

When Does NYT Connections Reset?

That’s today’s puzzle, but if you want the next one, the game resets daily at midnight local time (that’s your local time). You’ll have 24 hours to complete the puzzle when midnight ticks over in your timezone.

How did you do? Did you get a perfect run? Let me know what your streak looks like on the Insider Gaming Discord server

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Onimusha: Way of the Sword Launching Earlier Than Planned
item.content:encoded:
Capcom has confirmed on social media that Onimusha: Way of the Sword will now release almost three weeks earlier than planned, dropping on September 4th instead of September 24th. In the post, the team didn’t explain why they’d made this change, but fans welcomed it nonetheless.

They explained that the pre-order bonus will now become an ‘early adopter’ bonus, and users will have until September 24th to claim it. Additionally, the demo will be updated to reflect the change, but it won’t get any new content.

Onimusha Makes a Rare Move

It’s not often that games are brought forward these days, but it does happen. Earlier this year, PRAGMATA, another Capcom title, was released a week earlier than planned.

It’s not all good news, though. Capcom admitted that things are a little bit awkward for Nintendo Switch 2 players:

As a result of the release date change, pre-orders for the Nintendo Switch 2 digital version will be automatically canceled. You will need to place a new pre-order on Nintendo eShop. We apologize for any inconvenience caused.

Pre-orders for the physical edition, including those placed via e-Capcom, will not be cancelled.

Perhaps the relocation is something to do with Grand Theft Auto 6 launching just three weeks after the original release date. But now, Onimusha: Way of the Sword will need to closely contend with The Blood of Dawnwalker, a somewhat similar game that launches just one day earlier.

It might also lose some players just ten days later when Marvel’s Wolverine hits the market.

  • RELATED: Onimusha Release Date Might Be Moved

Are you excited about the earlier release date for Onimusha: Way of the Sword? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Bethesda made a clever post about physical games, and for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill
